textPlain: This was the strongest earthquake I felt since I moved to Bay Area ten years ago. Luckily, it was quite short. Woke me and all my friends in the SF and vicinity up though.
Seems like a minor earthquake.  Does it deserve top of HN?
My prayers to dismantle the AI industry have been answered
Second strongest I remember in my 16 years here, with the 2014 Napa Quake being notably more shaking.
Same. Been 12 years in SF and this was the strongest. We were awake as well so we felt the whole thing. I think I saw the walls move.Luckily it was short.
I usually don’t wake up to these but this one jolted me awake. I wish I had slept through this one though.
I would assume someone new to CA would find it concerning.It's fun to think about it - some Japanese people would move to CA just because of the more stable geology.
It's notable for people in California where a lot of HN users are based.
The votes seem to suggest it
It's gone sooner if you don't interact with the thread...
